name: leo-int-mc
purpose: Adapt INT-MC low-overhead telemetry recovery to predictable dynamic LEO satellite networks.

sampling.warmup_slices: auto: half of refresh_slices, at least 2
sampling.window_size: auto: about one LEO orbital period from stage-one step_minutes
sampling.prediction_horizon_slices: auto: about two orbital windows, capped by the run slice count
sampling.ml_epochs: defaults to iterations for machine-learning completion backends; research runs should increase this to 24-80 depending on constellation size
sampling.ml_latent_rank: auto: max(32, rank * 8), used by the CoSTCo-style backend
sampling.adaptive_probe_budget_enabled: default false for native INT-MC compatibility; enabled in enhanced LEO-INT-MC pass

leo_adaptations.contact_plan: Generate predicted active-link masks from stage-one orbital/link-budget parameters before INT-MC path selection.
leo_adaptations.contact_plan_outputs: predicted-contact-plan.json/csv/summary/windows/evaluation record horizon, refresh, rolling windows, precision, recall and per-slice topology error.
leo_adaptations.topology_classes: Reuse path templates across similar topology snapshots instead of recomputing every slice from scratch.
leo_adaptations.topology_similarity: Compare active links, inter-plane links, bottlenecks, routed paths and normalized link-state matrices before reusing a probe plan.
leo_adaptations.adaptive_threshold_explainability: For every slice, export the adaptive reuse threshold, reuse margin and per-factor tightening/relaxation terms: volatility, bottleneck, load, contact uncertainty, structural drift, link-state drift, route drift, OAM pressure, stability history and planning overhead pressure.
leo_adaptations.adaptive_threshold_calibration: Calibrate the reuse threshold from the current distribution of topology-class similarities and a short predicted future topology window, so reuse is allowed for similar topologies but tightened when class ambiguity or near-future drift is high.
leo_adaptations.topology_forecast_stability_window: For every slice, estimate how many future slices remain similar to the current active-link topology, when the next major drift is expected, and whether the selector should reuse, locally repair or preemptively replan.
leo_adaptations.forecast_aware_path_scoring: Score candidate probe paths with a rolling future contact window so links with predicted state transitions, near outages, contact scarcity or declining availability are sampled before they become stale or unreachable.
leo_adaptations.topology_versioned_active_telemetry_obje: Assign every exact active-link snapshot a topology version inside its reusable topology class, then conservatively rerank probe paths by a single normalized objective over telemetry bytes, telemetry energy, planning cost, expected residual reconstruction error and residual state age. Path information is uncertainty multiplied by predicted topology risk and metadata observation yield, minus marginal redundancy. Low-confidence evidence falls back to the legacy INT-MC ranking.
leo_adaptations.cost_aware_marginal_sampling: Estimate per-probe metadata bytes, report bytes, probe forwarding bytes and telemetry energy before ranking paths, then prefer high-value observations per KB under the same INT overhead contract.
leo_adaptations.soft_telemetry_byte_budget: Optionally enforce a per-slice telemetry byte budget during INT-MC path selection. Noncritical paths over budget are suppressed, while OAM retest, missing-coverage and near-outage paths can override the budget and record the overrun.
leo_adaptations.adaptive_probe_budget: When enabled, high-confidence topology reuse with a stable forecast window reduces the effective probe sampling rate, active-link target, per-slice probe path cap and telemetry byte pressure. OAM replan pressure, unknown-state pressure, low-confidence pressure or priority retest pressure escalates the same budget and can break reuse.
leo_adaptations.oam_path_cap_escalation: Enhanced LEO-INT-MC can temporarily raise the effective per-slice probe path cap above the native configured cap when Ground OAM reports high unknown, low-confidence or retest pressure. This makes OAM feedback a strong closed loop: confidence debt can produce more actual probe packets and wider coverage, while native INT-MC keeps the fixed cap.
leo_adaptations.adaptive_metadata_profile: Enhanced LEO-INT-MC can turn high-confidence topology reuse into compact INT metadata and path-only observation. Reused, stable and low-OAM-pressure slices carry fewer bytes per hop, suppress adjacent-link scans and generate smaller sink reports, while fresh replans, drift pressure and OAM retest pressure keep the full metadata and all-adjacent observation profile. Native INT-MC keeps the standard profile.
leo_adaptations.reuse_duplicate_probe_suppression: Under high-confidence topology reuse, low-risk cached probe paths that add no new link coverage and do not hit OAM/local-risk targets are suppressed before transmission. This turns reuse into actual telemetry communication savings, not only planning-cost savings.
leo_adaptations.local_adaptive_sampling: Within the per-slice budget, candidate paths crossing high-utilization, congested, high-queue, dropped-traffic, high-PER or low-availability links receive a local-risk priority bonus and can override soft telemetry suppression. Low-risk cached-reuse paths receive sparse-sampling treatment.
leo_adaptations.local_repair: Repair candidate probe paths on the predicted active graph when a future contact gap invalidates an old route.
leo_adaptations.active_observed_masks: Separate physically unavailable links from available-but-unobserved links.
leo_adaptations.explicit_sampling_mask: Export a slice-link probe sampling mask that distinguishes topology-down links, planned-sampled links and active-unobserved links left for completion.
leo_adaptations.completion_scope: Complete only active and unobserved links; topology-down links stay down.
leo_adaptations.node_completion_scope: Complete node CPU, queue, cache and energy states at Ground OAM from partial probe observations.
leo_adaptations.ground_oam_prior_estimates: Before matrix completion, Ground OAM can attach low-confidence prior estimates to never-observed nodes or links using same-slice and spatial-group statistics from delivered reports. These estimates are not counted as observed telemetry.
leo_adaptations.ground_oam_confidence_decay: Ground OAM stores state age, confidence before decay and exponential half-life decay factors for stale carry-over estimates.
leo_adaptations.ground_oam_conflict_fusion: Multiple delivered INT observations for the same node/link are fused with majority categorical values, mean numeric values and explicit categorical/numeric conflict severity that reduces confidence and drives retest pressure.
leo_adaptations.ground_oam_quality_feedback: Enhanced LEO-INT-MC can preserve Ground OAM fusion confidence and conflict severity when a node/link is directly observed. Low-quality direct observations no longer become full-confidence cells; they lower reconstruction confidence and can generate next-slice priority retest targets. Native INT-MC keeps this disabled.
leo_adaptations.multi_metric_link_completion: Complete link utilization, latency, capacity, congestion, queued traffic, dropped traffic and packet error rate.
leo_adaptations.metric_tensor_coupling: Enhanced LEO-INT-MC can treat the reconstructed link-time-metric cell as a compact tensor state instead of independent scalar matrices. For inferred links only, Ground OAM derives cross-metric pressure from utilization, route load, congestion, queue latency, packet error rate and drops, then raises or caps inconsistent queue/congestion/loss estimates. The layer uses reconstructed metrics and routed-task metadata, never hidden link truth, and native INT-MC keeps it disabled.
leo_adaptations.node_state_coupling: Enhanced LEO-INT-MC can apply a Ground OAM post-completion coupling layer over inferred node metrics. It derives node workload from routed task paths, then couples CPU, queue depth, queued traffic, cache usage and energy under sunlight/shadow context. Directly observed node INT records are left unchanged, and native INT-MC keeps this disabled.
leo_adaptations.joint_state_coupling: Enhanced LEO-INT-MC can apply a Ground OAM post-completion coupling layer that enforces coarse consistency between reconstructed utilization, route load, queue latency, congestion, packet error rate, queued traffic and drops. The layer uses reconstructed metrics plus routed traffic metadata, not hidden truth. It also caps implausible inferred queue-latency outliers caused by unbounded low-rank extrapolation while leaving directly observed INT values unchanged.
leo_adaptations.state_tensor_joint_completion: Enhanced LEO-INT-MC can apply an auditable post-completion state-tensor layer for inferred links. It treats utilization, route load, queue latency, congestion, queued traffic, drops and packet error rate as one coupled link-state vector, derives a joint pressure from reconstructed metrics plus routed-task metadata, and raises inconsistent queue/congestion/loss estimates without reading hidden stage-one truth. Native INT-MC keeps this disabled.
leo_adaptations.parallel_completion_backends: Eight independent single-metric Ground OAM backends coexist: structural prior-only, original low-rank, Soft-Impute, offline Kalman/RTS smoothing, same-slice orbit-graph neighbor interpolation, graph-temporal regularized completion, ST-GNN and CoSTCo-style tensor completion. They share the same INT sampling, observation masks, output schema and truth-only evaluation pipeline, so experiment 3 changes only the completion method.
leo_adaptations.prior_only_completion: A no-learning weak baseline that returns the observable temporal/orbit-neighbor/spatial-group initialization while locking direct observations and topology-down cells. It quantifies how much accuracy comes from initialization before any matrix learner runs.
leo_adaptations.soft_impute_completion: A prior-initialized Soft-Impute backend that repeatedly applies singular-value soft thresholding as a nuclear-norm proximal step, then restores directly observed cells and the active topology mask.
leo_adaptations.kalman_smoother_completion: An offline local-level Kalman plus Rauch-Tung-Striebel smoother over each active object segment. It uses only delivered observations from the evaluation window, locks direct observations, and is explicitly not reported as an online-causal method.
leo_adaptations.graph_neighbor_completion: A non-parametric same-slice orbit-graph interpolation baseline. It diffuses values over active graph neighbors while anchoring the structural prior, without temporal or low-rank terms.
leo_adaptations.graph_regularized_completion: A graph-temporal regularized backend that updates only missing cells from same-slice orbit-graph neighbors, adjacent time slices, the structural initialization and a soft-threshold low-rank target. Direct observations and topology-down cells are locked after every Jacobi iteration.
leo_adaptations.st_gnn_completion: A research-scale single-metric spatio-temporal graph completer. It learns a multi-layer residual MLP from delivered INT observations, combines temporal trend features, attention-weighted tensor neighbors, row/column observation statistics and orbit-plane/slot context, then performs message passing over the active LEO topology at Ground OAM.
leo_adaptations.costco_completion: A research-scale single-metric coordinate tensor completer inspired by CoSTCo. It learns high-rank link/node latent embeddings, time-slice embeddings and a nonlinear coordinate-context MLP from delivered INT observations, then fills active unobserved matrix cells without touching topology-down cells.
leo_adaptations.queue_latency_alignment: INT hop records export observed_link_queue_latency_ms as a queue-drain delay estimate, so satellite INT can be compared with INT-MC queue_latency without using queued traffic as a proxy.
leo_adaptations.temporal_context_completion: Matrix completion initializes missing values with observed neighbor slices, same-slice spatial-group statistics, historical spatial groups, then low-rank reconstruction; confidence is adjusted by contact availability, sunlight/shadow and power margin context.
leo_adaptations.orbit_periodic_prior: Enhanced LEO-INT-MC can initialize missing link/node cells from already delivered observations of the same link or node at orbital-period lagged time slices. This exploits predictable LEO periodicity without reading hidden stage-one truth, and native INT-MC keeps it disabled.
leo_adaptations.explainable_context_prior_tags: Reconstructed link/node rows export context_prior_tags and context_prior_risk for polar/high-latitude regions, shadow/sunlight, solar interference, low availability, near range limit, congestion and business hotspots. These tags explain the confidence context without filling unknowns from hidden truth.
leo_adaptations.tensor_neighbor_completion: Use same-slice observed neighbors in the plane-slot-time tensor, including adjacent slots in the same plane and same-slot neighbors in adjacent planes. When both temporal and tensor priors are available, use a 0.7 temporal / 0.3 tensor blend. These priors use only delivered OAM observations, not hidden truth metrics.
leo_adaptations.orbit_graph_regularization: Enhanced LEO-INT-MC can apply a Ground OAM post-completion graph regularization layer over plane-slot neighbors. It gently smooths inferred link metrics toward same-slice same-plane/adjacent-plane neighbors and exports regularized metric names, neighbor counts and strength. Native INT-MC keeps this disabled.
leo_adaptations.business_hotspot_tags: Attach routed-task traffic and path-hotspot features to reconstructed links without using hidden link truth metrics as telemetry.
leo_adaptations.business_hotspot_migration_prior: Enhanced LEO-INT-MC can infer a low-confidence migrating business hotspot from routed paths on the same link in adjacent time slices and from same-slice/orbit-neighbor links in the plane-slot tensor. The prior only uses route metadata already available at Ground OAM, never hidden link utilization, queue or loss truth, and native INT-MC keeps it disabled.
leo_adaptations.overhead_accounting: Export per-slice INT bytes/energy, per-link telemetry forwarding load and per-node telemetry processing/downlink energy so native probe-int and LEO-INT-MC can be compared under the same overhead contract.
leo_adaptations.energy_guard: Use battery level, power-saving mode, sunlight/shadow, solar exposure and power margin to suppress nonessential probes while preserving critical coverage.
leo_adaptations.energy_aware_sampling_budget: Adjust the effective per-slice probe sampling rate from predicted path energy risk, shadow ratio, low-energy nodes, low solar exposure and power deficit; OAM critical coverage pressure can partially restore sampling to avoid missing important anomalies.
leo_adaptations.ground_oam_feedback: Optionally feed ground-oam-priority-retest.csv from a previous OAM reconstruction into INT-MC path selection so low-confidence, stale, unknown or warning targets bias future probes.
leo_adaptations.ground_oam_replan_gate: Convert accumulated Ground OAM uncertainty into an OAM replan pressure; if the pressure exceeds the threshold, the selector refreshes the probe plan even when topology similarity would otherwise allow reuse.
leo_adaptations.ground_oam_control_actions: Export per-slice Ground OAM control actions from OAM-estimated unknown, stale, prior, low-confidence, conflict, downlink and retest pressure. These actions recommend maintaining the current plan, scheduling priority retests or refreshing the probe plan without using stage-one truth.
leo_adaptations.ground_oam_control_closed_loop: The INT-MC path selector can consume ground-oam-control-actions.csv from a previous run to bias probe paths toward OAM target nodes/links or force fresh replanning when OAM control pressure is high.
leo_adaptations.ground_oam_next_slice_control: Ground OAM control actions are applied to next_slice_index when present, so low-confidence, stale, unknown or conflicting state detected at slice t can force coverage and budget escalation at slice t+1. Legacy actions without next_slice_index still apply to their own slice for compatibility.
leo_adaptations.ground_oam_adaptive_budget: Ground OAM control actions also recommend per-slice sampling rates, active-link sampling targets, telemetry byte budgets and downlink budgets from OAM confidence debt, unknown/stale pressure, conflict pressure, retest pressure and downlink pressure. When supplied to the selector, these recommendations close the loop between OAM state quality and low-overhead probe planning.
leo_adaptations.ground_oam_mandatory_coverage: Enhanced LEO-INT-MC treats OAM priority retest nodes/links as mandatory next-round coverage targets. If a topology-reuse cached plan does not cover them, the selector breaks reuse and replans from fresh candidates; under telemetry budget pressure, paths hitting OAM targets are treated as critical coverage overrides.
leo_adaptations.importance_aware_selective_metadata: A causal rolling target model ranks nodes and links from lagged Ground OAM uncertainty, state age, observed volatility, known routed workload, predictable contact risk, state criticality and orbit-plane exploration. The selector preserves the normal base probe plan and may append only a bounded repair set backed by mandatory OAM demand, positive AoI debt or strong criticality evidence. Per-hop masks choose full, core, light or forward-only metadata; a path reaching either endpoint may additionally collect a specifically nominated local link without exposing unrelated adjacent links. AoI debt supplies a conservative repair-path lower bound, omitted fields stay unknown at Ground OAM, and probe, report, target-mask and emitted metadata bytes are all included in actual overhead.
leo_adaptations.ground_oam_pressure_budget_derivation: If Ground OAM has high pressure but does not provide explicit sampling recommendations, the enhanced selector derives a conservative sampling/target increase from the pressure fields instead of treating OAM as score-only metadata.
leo_adaptations.planning_overhead_accounting: Estimate normalized ground-side planning cost for full per-slice replanning versus cached topology reuse plus local repair.
leo_adaptations.execution_location: Matrix completion runs at Ground OAM, not on satellites.
